meaning: adj.(法国数学家、力学家)拉格朗日的,与拉格朗日有关的n.=Lagrangian function
examples: The probe will stay near an area called a Lagrangian point - there are five similar points.
We prove that the Lagrangian dual bound is identical to the lower bound obtained by convex relaxation.
The particle track technology is applied to establish the three-dimensional Lagrangian residual current model.
It allows him (or, rather, his computer) to see the ever-shifting Lagrangian pattern in the bay.
Lagrangian analysis might allow the behaviour of spills to be predicted more easily.
